issuesFound.title: Missing page title
issuesFound.explanation: The <title> element is present but empty. Screen reader users rely on the page title to understand the page's purpose and distinguish it from other pages.
issuesFound.fix: Line 7: <title>Luminary Studio — Accessible design and productivity tools for every team</title>

issuesFound.title: Non-descriptive link text
issuesFound.explanation: The link contains only "Read more" as visible text, which provides no context about the destination when read out of sequence by screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 169: Start free trial (no credit card required)

issuesFound.title: Image missing alt attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The img element for the hero dashboard image is missing an alt attribute. All images must have an alt attribute, either with descriptive text or explicitly empty for decorative images.
issuesFound.fix: Line 203:

issuesFound.title: Form control missing accessible label
issuesFound.explanation: The email input field lacks an accessible label. Users of assistive technologies need programmatic labels to understand form controls.
issuesFound.fix: Line 746: <input type="email" id="contact-email" name="email" autocomplete="work email" required aria-required="true" aria-label="Your email address">

issuesFound.title: Toggle button missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The navigation toggle button controls the visibility of the main navigation menu but lacks aria-expanded to communicate its state to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 31: <button type="button" class="nav-toggle" aria-expanded="false" aria-controls="nav-menu">

issuesFound.title: Table header missing scope
issuesFound.explanation: The th element lacks a scope attribute, making it unclear whether it is a row or column header. This affects how screen readers announce table data.
issuesFound.fix: Line 943: <th scope="col">Feature</th>

issuesFound.title: Non‑descriptive link text “Read more”
issuesFound.explanation: The link’s visible text is “Read more”, which does not convey sufficient purpose to users of assistive technology. This affects all users who rely on link names to understand navigation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 126: Read more about Luminary Studio(no credit card required)

issuesFound.title: Non‑descriptive link text “Click here”
issuesFound.explanation: The link’s visible text is “Click here”, providing no information about the link’s purpose. This hinders navigation for screen‑reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 364: View Contrast Studio details

issuesFound.title: Button missing accessible name (search submit)
issuesFound.explanation: The search form’s submit button contains only a decorative image with empty alt text and provides no accessible name, making it invisible to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 94:

issuesFound.title: Table header missing scope
issuesFound.explanation: The <th> element in the comparison table lacks a scope attribute, preventing assistive technologies from correctly associating header cells with data cells.
issuesFound.fix: Line 417: <th scope="col">Feature</th>

issuesFound.title: Heading level skip (h2 → h5)
issuesFound.explanation: After an <h2> (“How Luminary Studio works”), the next heading is an <h5> (“Connect your project”), skipping heading levels h3 and h4. This disrupts the hierarchical structure for screen‑reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Change the heading to <h3> (or add intervening <h3>/<h4> headings) so the hierarchy is continuous.

issuesFound.title: Form control missing accessible label (newsletter email)
issuesFound.explanation: The email input in the newsletter form has no associated <label>, aria‑label, or aria‑labelledby, leaving screen‑reader users without a name for the field.
issuesFound.fix: Line 86: <input id="newsletter-email" name="email" type="email" autocomplete="email" required aria-required="true" placeholder="you@example.com" aria-label="Email address">

issuesFound.title: Form control missing accessible label (contact email)
issuesFound.explanation: The contact form’s email input lacks a <label> or aria‑label, so assistive technologies cannot convey its purpose.
issuesFound.fix: Line 559: <input id="contact-email" name="email" type="email" autocomplete="work email" required aria-required="true" aria-label="Email address">

issuesFound.title: Image missing alt attribute (hero illustration)
issuesFound.explanation: The hero image has no alt attribute, providing no alternative text for users who cannot see the image.
issuesFound.fix: Line 141:

issuesFound.title: Image missing alt attribute (Nova Health logo)
issuesFound.explanation: The logo image lacks an alt attribute, so screen‑reader users receive no description of the brand.
issuesFound.fix: Line 155:

issuesFound.title: Image missing alt attribute (Contrast Studio product image)
issuesFound.explanation: The product image for Contrast Studio has no alt attribute, leaving it inaccessible to users of assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Line 354:

issuesFound.title: Landmark <nav> missing distinguishing label (main navigation)
issuesFound.explanation: The primary navigation <nav id="main-nav"> lacks an aria‑label or aria‑labelledby, making it indistinguishable from other navigation landmarks for screen‑reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 36: <nav id="main-nav" aria-label="Primary navigation">

issuesFound.title: Landmark <nav> missing distinguishing label (account / CTA navigation)
issuesFound.explanation: The secondary <nav> containing login and signup links has no aria‑label or aria‑labelledby, preventing users from differentiating it from other navigation regions.
issuesFound.fix: Line 100: <nav aria-label="Account navigation">

issuesFound.title: Landmark <nav> missing distinguishing label (footer Products navigation)
issuesFound.explanation: The footer navigation for “Products” lacks an aria‑label or aria‑labelledby, so screen‑reader users cannot identify its purpose among multiple footer navs.
issuesFound.fix: Line 514: <nav aria-label="Products navigation">

issuesFound.title: Landmark <nav> missing distinguishing label (footer Resources navigation)
issuesFound.explanation: The footer navigation for “Resources” has no aria‑label or aria‑labelledby, making it indistinguishable from other footer navigation landmarks.
issuesFound.fix: Line 527: <nav aria-label="Resources navigation">

issuesFound.title: Landmark <nav> missing distinguishing label (footer Company navigation)
issuesFound.explanation: The footer navigation for “Company” lacks an aria‑label or aria‑labelledby, preventing clear identification for assistive‑technology users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 540: <nav aria-label="Company navigation">

issuesFound.title: Landmark <nav> missing distinguishing label (footer Legal navigation)
issuesFound.explanation: The footer navigation for “Legal” lacks an aria‑label or aria‑labelledby, making it ambiguous for screen‑reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 551: <nav aria-label="Legal navigation">

issuesFound.title: Landmark <section> missing distinguishing label (hero section)
issuesFound.explanation: The hero <section> does not have an aria‑label or aria‑labelledby, so it cannot be distinguished from other sections by assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 117: <section class="hero" aria-label="Hero">

issuesFound.title: Landmark <section> missing distinguishing label (logo‑bar section)
issuesFound.explanation: The logo‑bar <section> lacks an aria‑label or aria‑labelledby, making it indistinguishable from other sections for screen‑reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 151: <section class="logo-bar" aria-label="Partner logos">

issuesFound.title: Broken ARIA reference (features‑section aria‑labelledby)
issuesFound.explanation: The <section class="features-section"> references aria‑labelledby="features-heading", but no element with id="features-heading" exists, so the label cannot be resolved for assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 166:

Everything you need to ship accessible software

issuesFound.title: Input missing autocomplete attribute (first name)
issuesFound.explanation: The “First name” input does not have an autocomplete attribute, missing an opportunity to help users fill personal data quickly. This affects keyboard and screen‑reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 537: <input type="text" id="contact-first-name" name="first_name" required autocomplete="given-name">

fpTitles: Landmark <nav> missing distinguishing label (footer Resources navigation)
Landmark <nav> missing distinguishing label (footer Company navigation)
Landmark <nav> missing distinguishing label (footer Legal navigation)

issuesFound.title: Missing page title
issuesFound.explanation: The <title> element is empty, which means screen reader users cannot identify the page content or purpose. This violates WCAG 2.4.2 (Page Titled).
issuesFound.fix: Line 7: <title>Luminary Studio — Accessible design and productivity tools</title>

issuesFound.title: Missing skip link
issuesFound.explanation: There is no skip link to bypass repeated navigation and jump directly to main content. Keyboard users must tab through every header link on every page load, violating WCAG 2.4.1 (Bypass Blocks).
issuesFound.fix: Line 33: Skip to main content

issuesFound.title: Button missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The nav-toggle button has no accessible name. It contains only an aria-hidden span, so screen readers announce nothing. Users cannot identify its purpose.
issuesFound.fix: Line 48-51: <button type="button" class="nav-toggle" aria-label="Open main menu">

issuesFound.title: Link with non-descriptive text
issuesFound.explanation: The link text "Read more" is non-descriptive and does not convey the destination or purpose. This forces screen reader users to explore context, violating WCAG 2.4.4 (Link Purpose).
issuesFound.fix: Line 128: Start free trial

issuesFound.title: Image missing alt attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The hero dashboard image has no alt attribute, so screen readers announce nothing. Users cannot understand the visual content.
issuesFound.fix: Line 135-139:

issuesFound.title: Button missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The FAQ toggle button controls expandable content (dd) but has no aria-expanded attribute. Screen readers cannot announce the current state (expanded/collapsed).
issuesFound.fix: Line 507-510: <button type="button" aria-expanded="false" id="faq-1-question">

issuesFound.title: Input missing autocomplete
issuesFound.explanation: The first name input collects personal data (name) but has no autocomplete attribute. This violates WCAG 1.3.5 (Identify Input Purpose).
issuesFound.fix: Line 613-616: <input type="text" id="contact-first-name" name="first_name" autocomplete="given-name" required>

issuesFound.title: Landmark missing distinguishing label
issuesFound.explanation: There are two <nav> landmarks on the page. The second <nav> (lines 53-83) has no aria-label or aria-labelledby, so assistive technology users cannot distinguish it from the first.
issuesFound.fix: Line 53-83: <nav aria-label="Account navigation">
